majors.count_method: counted distinct Yale College major entries listed on Yale's official 'Majors in Yale College' catalog page
titles: History of Science, Medicine, and Public Health
Russian, East European, and Eurasian Studies
majors.notes: titles extracted from Yale's official Yale College Programs of Study majors page; degree suffixes were removed from the published major labels while keeping Yale's official major names.

admissions.testing_policy: Yale uses a test-flexible policy that requires first-year applicants to submit scores from one or more of the following: ACT, AP, IB, or SAT.
admissions.gpa_policy: Yale does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited admissions pages.
admissions.course_rigor: The cited Yale admissions pages do not provide a single minimum course-rigor formula.
admissions.recommendations: Yale requires letters of recommendation from two teachers and one counselor, plus a school report with transcript.
admissions.essays: Yale requires Yale-specific short essays for first-year applicants.

projects_research: Yale allows applicants to submit supplementary material showcasing STEM research.

evidence.claim: Yale has 82 official undergraduate major/program titles on the cited official catalog page.

evidence.claim: Yale requires letters of recommendation from two teachers and one counselor, plus a school report with transcript.

evidence.claim: Yale requires Yale-specific short essays for first-year applicants.
evidence.source_excerpt: First-year applicants complete Yale-specific short essays. Review the 2025-2026 essay topics for all applications.

evidence.claim: Yale allows applicants to submit supplementary material showcasing STEM research.
evidence.source_excerpt: Supplements Applicants may submit supplementary material showcasing Visual Art, Dance, Music, Film, or STEM research.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Yale University' (legacyId=1222) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (82 titles).
Phase 4 structured upgrade applied to top-10 expansion schools.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.
Yale major titles were manually repaired from the official Yale College Programs of Study majors page.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Yale University' (legacyId=1222) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Yale News, Yale Alumni Association, and Yale school-domain stories, prioritizing alumni-specific profiles or official university news items with explicit degree-year evidence and concrete professional or civic impact.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official yale.edu and alumni.yale.edu story, profile, and interview pages.

items.name: Mohamed Diallo
items.bio: Yale Library says Mohamed Diallo ’26 is using archival research and interactive mapping to reconstruct the history of Yale’s early Black students.
items.description: Student researcher and digital-history project builder.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Hadas Dabas
items.bio: Yale GSAS says Hadas Dabas ’25 PhD was named a 2026 Schmidt Science Fellow and plans to pivot from genetics to climate-focused fungal engineering.
items.description: Schmidt Science Fellow and genetics PhD alumna.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Anna Bargman
items.bio: Yale News says Anna Bargman ’25, after captaining Yale women’s hockey, reunited professionally with teammate Elle Hartje on the New York Sirens roster.
items.description: Professional hockey player and former Yale captain.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Yale News feature with explicit class year and substantive professional update on a shared alumni profile page.

items.name: Elle Hartje
items.bio: Yale News says Elle Hartje ’24 captained Yale women’s hockey before joining the New York Sirens in the Professional Women’s Hockey League.
items.description: Professional hockey player and former Yale captain.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Yale News feature with explicit class year and substantive professional update on a shared alumni profile page.

items.name: Conor Rooney
items.bio: Yale Chemistry says Conor Rooney ’24 PhD returned to campus as keynote speaker after launching Oxylus Energy to commercialize carbon-conversion technology developed during his doctoral work.
items.description: Climate-tech founder and chemistry PhD alumnus.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Jinali Mody
items.bio: Yale News says Jinali Mody ’23 MEM, founder and CEO of a startup making plant-based leather from crop waste, was named a 2025 UN Young Champion of the Earth.
items.description: Climate-tech founder making plant-based leather.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Kimberly Hill
items.bio: Yale Divinity School says Kimberly Hill ’00 MAR wrote the romantic comedy play Joy Meets Girl while weaving in scriptural themes beneath its humor.
items.description: Playwright and Yale Divinity alumna.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Derek Chang
items.bio: Yale News says Derek Chang ’89, president and CEO of Liberty Media, was appointed a successor trustee of the Yale Corporation.
items.description: Media executive and Yale Corporation successor trustee.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Yale News story with explicit class year and leadership role on a shared trustee-appointment page.

items.name: Philippe Costeletos
items.bio: Yale News says Philippe Costeletos ’87, founder of Stemar Capital Partners, was appointed a successor trustee of the Yale Corporation.
items.description: Investor and Yale Corporation successor trustee.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Yale News story with explicit class year and leadership role on a shared trustee-appointment page.

items.name: Min Jin Lee
items.bio: Yale News says Min Jin Lee ’90, a celebrated novelist, essayist, editor, and critic, returned to campus as the 2026 Class Day speaker.
items.description: Novelist, essayist, and critic.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Melissa Matthes
items.bio: Yale Divinity School says Melissa Matthes ’09 MDiv teaches religion and ethics at the U.S. Coast Guard Academy and brings a strong sense of mission and service to her work.
items.description: Educator in religion and ethics at a U.S. service academy.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Joe Tsai
items.bio: Yale Ventures says Joe Tsai ’86, ’90 JD, co-founder and chairman of Alibaba Group, was chosen to keynote the 2026 Yale Innovation Summit.
items.description: Alibaba co-founder and business leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Bob Rifkin
items.bio: Yale Insights says Bob Rifkin ’89, board president of the Belmont Voice, is helping rebuild local news through a nonprofit community-journalism model.
items.description: Nonprofit local-news leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Spencer Reece
items.bio: Yale Divinity School says the Rev. Spencer Reece ’11 MDiv is a priest and poet whose recent sermon reflected on church, community, and blessing.
items.description: Priest and poet.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Jill Lepore
items.bio: Yale News says historian Jill Lepore ’95 PhD used her Tanner Lectures at Yale to examine fears of an ‘artificial state’ shaped by technology and science fiction.
items.description: Historian and public intellectual.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Josh Geballe
items.bio: Yale Alumni Association says Josh Geballe ’97, ’02 MBA serves as Yale’s senior associate provost for entrepreneurship and innovation and managing director of Yale Ventures.
items.description: Entrepreneurship and innovation leader at Yale.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Linda Rottenberg
items.bio: Yale Ventures says Linda Rottenberg ’93 built Endeavor to back ambitious entrepreneurs and prove that talent is everywhere.
items.description: Endeavor co-founder and entrepreneurship leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
items.bio: Yale MacMillan Center says Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie ’08 returned to Yale to discuss writing craft and the responsibility of shaping narrative.
items.description: Internationally acclaimed novelist and essayist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Kristrún Frostadóttir
items.bio: Yale Jackson School says Kristrún Frostadóttir MA ’16 returned to Yale as Iceland’s prime minister and spoke about the country’s strengths and policy direction.
items.description: Prime minister of Iceland.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Caroline Nguyen
items.bio: Yale Alumni Association says Caroline Nguyen ’13 leads the AAAYA Southern California chapter and uses Lunar New Year gatherings to strengthen cross-generational Yale alumni connections.
items.description: Alumni-community leader and AAAYA chapter head.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

warnings: Refreshed stale professor entries to backfill required bio/confirmation fields.
Professor bios and confirmation links currently come from RateMyProfessors-derived profiles; a school-official faculty-page resolver still needs to be implemented for stronger verification.
